seo_h1: Boeo of Delphi on the Founding of the Oracle

seo.browser_title: Boeo of Delphi Ancient Poet on Eulogikon Greek Archive
seo.meta_title: Boeo of Delphi Ancient Greek Epic Poetry Fragments
seo.meta_description: Boeo of Delphi, a Hellenistic era poet from the 6th century BCE. Surviving fragments of epic verse. Free to read and download from the Greek library archive.

biography_html:
Boeo (or Boio) of Delphi was a poetess from Delphi, likely active in the 6th century BCE. She is known solely from a later account by Pausanias, which identifies her as the author of a hexameter poem on the founding of the Delphic oracle. No biographical details survive.

Her only attested work is the lost hexameter poem Foundation of the Oracle at Delphi, cited by Pausanias. It presented an alternative foundation myth where Apollo was received by a man named Cretines, differing from the standard narrative involving the serpent Python.

Boeo is significant as a rare named female poet from the early Greek tradition. Her work preserves a distinct, possibly local Delphic version of the oracle's origins, contrasting with the dominant Panhellenic myth. Her existence, attested only through fragmentary transmission, highlights the limited traces of women's contributions to early epic poetry.
